config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan atas nama “kepentingan সমাজ” secara umum, karena itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas untuk menekan kelompok yang lemah. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan membatasi kekuasaan secara adil, bukan dengan menginjak hak dasar orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati terlebih dahulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dirumuskan dari sana.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that idea can easily become an excuse for suppressing ordinary people. From a Chinese perspective, I understand why many people value social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, especially after seeing how disorder can hurt everyone, but that does not justify ignoring basic rights. In my view, the real standard should be whether a policy genuinely protects the public without giving too much unchecked power to authorities or the majority. If individual rights are treated as secondary in principle, they can disappear in practice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, menekan suara minoritas, atau mengorbankan orang kecil yang tidak punya perlindungan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, karena kalau hak individu dianggap sekunder, pada praktiknya yang kuat akan selalu menang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang adil justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu kepentingan kolektif dirumuskan tanpa melanggar martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because that wording is too easy to turn into a blank check for power. From my perspective in China, people do value order, stability, and collective responsibility, and those things are not meaningless, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can be used to silence criticism or ignore ordinary people’s rights. I would rather say society and the individual need balance, with basic rights acting as a hard limit that public policy should not cross. If individual rights are only protected when convenient, then society may look strong on the surface while becoming unjust underneath.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering jadi pintu masuk untuk penggusuran, pembungkaman kritik, atau keputusan sepihak yang merugikan orang kecil, jadi saya sangat curiga pada klaim seperti itu. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun di atas batas yang tegas: hak dasar individu tidak boleh dijadikan korban pertama. Kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan kapan saja atas nama kolektif, maka yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang makin liar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general principle. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize order, stability, and collective responsibility, but I also know how easily “the common good” can become a justification for ignoring ordinary people, especially when power is not well checked. The strongest argument from the other side is that society does need some shared priorities, but those priorities should never override basic rights by default. A good society is not one that asks individuals to sacrifice everything first; it is one that protects rights while still solving collective problems fairly.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
